scenarios.description: charset(Charset) with xml syntax and first child not XmlDeclaration prepends a new xml declaration before existing element
scenarios.precondition: Document created via createShell("http://ex") with default html children, then outputSettings.syntax(xml)
scenarios.expectedResult: New XmlDeclaration named 'xml' prepended; existing html element shifts to index 1; encoding='CHARSET', version='1.0'
pseudocode: // Precondition: first child is an Element (not XmlDeclaration)
// Assert new XmlDeclaration at index 0 with name 'xml', encoding=cs.displayName(), version='1.0'
// Existing html element is now at index 1

scenarios.description: charset(Charset) with xml syntax and existing XmlDeclaration with non-'xml' name prepends a new xml declaration and leaves old one
scenarios.precondition: Document instantiated, prependChild(new XmlDeclaration("foo", false)), then outputSettings.syntax(xml)
scenarios.expectedResult: New XmlDeclaration at index 0 with name 'xml'; original 'foo' declaration moves to index 1; encoding='CHARSET', version='1.0'
pseudocode: // Precondition: first child is existing XmlDeclaration name!='xml'
// New XmlDeclaration at 0, old moves to 1
// Old declaration remains at index 1 unchanged name but version attr not '0.9'? (version attr unchanged)
